title: Robust Object Detection with Uncertainty-Based Attack Detection
summary: Object detection pipeline with Monte Carlo Dropout uncertainty estimation to flag adversarial and out-of-distribution inputs at inference time.
body:
## Problem

Modern object detection models are vulnerable to adversarial attacks (FGSM, PGD) that introduce imperceptible perturbations causing confident, incorrect predictions. Detecting these at inference time is critical for real-world deployment.

## Approach

Designed an object detection pipeline with Monte Carlo Dropout uncertainty estimation. At inference, multiple stochastic forward passes generate a distribution of predictions — high variance signals potential adversarial or out-of-distribution inputs, triggering a flag before predictions are acted upon.

## Results

Benchmarked against FGSM and PGD attacks; demonstrated measurable robustness improvement over baseline models. Uncertainty thresholding correctly identified adversarial samples with high recall.
